bead.title: agent executor: enforce Linux parent-death cleanup for execute-bead harness children
bead.description:
PROBLEM
When a `ddx work` worker dies abnormally while an execute-bead harness is running, the direct harness child can reparent to PID 1 and continue burning a harness session. This was observed on 2026-05-27 with a `claude --print` execute-bead child orphaned under PPID 1 for about 9h53m.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/agent/executor.go:156 spawns the harness with exec.Command(binary, args...) and then calls cmdSetProcessGroup(cmd).
- cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go:13-15 sets only SysProcAttr{Setpgid: true}; it does not configure a parent-death signal.
- cli/cmd/root.go:49-54 handles os.Interrupt and SIGTERM only. SIGKILL, crashes, OOM, and parent process death cannot run the normal defer/stopProcess cleanup.
- cli/internal/agent/executor.go:190-194 and cli/internal/agent/executor.go:231-255 only kill the process group when graceful executor paths run.

PROPOSED FIX
- On Linux, extend cmdSetProcessGroup in cli/internal/agent/executor_unix.go so execute-bead harness children receive a kernel parent-death signal, preferably SIGKILL unless implementation chooses SIGTERM with a defensible reason.
- Handle the Go runtime thread-migration caveat for SysProcAttr.Pdeathsig, for example by locking the OS thread around process start or by an equivalent parent-PID guard that makes the death signal reliable.
- Add a non-Linux fallback/rationale in platform-specific code or docs/tests so macOS and Windows behavior is explicit.
- Preserve the existing Setpgid behavior because graceful shutdown still relies on process-group termination.

NON-SCOPE
- Do not implement startup orphan reaping in this child; that is covered by the startup reaper child bead and cross-ref ddx-8f2e0ebf.
- Do not change docker-clone backend cleanup; that is covered by the docker-clone child bead.
- Graceful SIGINT/SIGTERM shutdown already works through executor.go:231-255; do not regress it.
- The harness-runner-internal leak in fizeau is covered by fizeau-50b6f055; this child remains at the ddx orchestrator -> harness-child boundary.

GOVERNING ARTIFACTS / CROSS-REFS
- docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md
- docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md
- fizeau-50b6f055
- ddx-8f2e0ebf
bead.acceptance:
1. TestOSExecutorExecuteInDir_LinuxPdeathsigKillsHarnessOnParentSIGKILL starts an execute-bead executor through a helper process, runs a fake long-lived harness, SIGKILLs the helper process, and asserts the direct harness child does not survive as a PID-1 orphan within a bounded grace on Linux.
2. TestCmdSetProcessGroup_LinuxConfiguresPdeathsigAndSetpgid verifies cmdSetProcessGroup preserves Setpgid and configures a Linux parent-death signal.
3. TestOSExecutorExecuteInDir_ParentDeathSignalThreadGuard verifies or documents through an explicit test/rationale that the Pdeathsig thread-migration caveat is handled by LockOSThread or an equivalent guard.
4. TestOSExecutorExecuteInDir_NonLinuxParentDeathFallbackDocumentsGap verifies non-Linux behavior is explicitly covered by platform-specific implementation, skip, or documented fallback rationale.
5. TestOSExecutorExecuteInDir_GracefulCancellationStillKillsProcessGroup is a regression guard showing ctx cancellation or SIGTERM-style graceful shutdown still terminates the harness process group.
6. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... -run 'Test(OSExecutorExecuteInDir_LinuxPdeathsigKillsHarnessOnParentSIGKILL|CmdSetProcessGroup_LinuxConfiguresPdeathsigAndSetpgid|OSExecutorExecuteInDir_ParentDeathSignalThreadGuard|OSExecutorExecuteInDir_NonLinuxParentDeathFallbackDocumentsGap|OSExecutorExecuteInDir_GracefulCancellationStillKillsProcessGroup)' -count=1 green.
7. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... green.
8. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
